title: Container name is not correct when use RESTful API
body:
### Issue Description

Container name is not correct when use RESTful API

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create Container with "http://libpod/containers/create"

```
root@podman:/home/podman/simple-demo# curl --unix-socket /tmp/podman.sock -X POST   -H "Content-Type: application/json"   -d '{
    "name": "test1",
    "Image": "swr.cn-north-4.myhuaweicloud.com/ddn-k8s/gcr.io/iguazio/alpine:3.20"    }'   http://libpod/containers/create
{"Id":"00935f1c0dff566156b28e3d0aac83d5ede7bc5068d3b4e3136460aee632709b","Warnings":[]}
root@podman:/home/podman/simple-demo# podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                                                COMMAND     CREATED        STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
00935f1c0dff  swr.cn-north-4.myhuaweicloud.com/ddn-k8s/gcr.io/iguazio/alpine:3.20  /bin/sh     4 seconds ago  Created                 fervent_jones
```
the name in json body is **test1**, but it created as random value  **fervent_jones**



2.Create Container with "http://d/v5.0.0/libpod/containers/create"

```
root@podman:/home/podman/simple-demo# curl --unix-socket /tmp/podman.sock -X POST   -H "Content-Type: application/json"   -d '{
    "name": "test1",
    "Image": "swr.cn-north-4.myhuaweicloud.com/ddn-k8s/gcr.io/iguazio/alpine:3.20"    }'   http://d/v5.0.0/libpod/containers/create
{"Id":"a5aa572275e5cdbcf2f928f0adb3481bc8dd07f497c0e7d3d8dd5b64a746fd89","Warnings":[]}
root@podman:/home/podman/simple-demo# podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                                                COMMAND     CREATED        STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
a5aa572275e5  swr.cn-north-4.myhuaweicloud.com/ddn-k8s/gcr.io/iguazio/alpine:3.20  /bin/sh     6 seconds ago  Created                 test1
```
the name in json body is **test1**

**but if i use http://d/v5.0.0/libpod/containers/create, my macvlan network interface will not be correct generated when create container**
```
podman network create \
  --driver macvlan \
  --subnet 192.168.1.0/24 \
  --gateway 192.168.1.1 \
  --ip-range 192.168.1.128/25 \
  --opt parent=ens33 \
  test_macvlan
```

```
root@podman:/home/podman/simple-demo# curl --unix-socket /tmp/podman.sock -X POST \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "test1",
    "Image": "swr.cn-north-4.myhuaweicloud.com/ddn-k8s/gcr.io/iguazio/alpine:3.20",
    "NetworkingConfig": {
      "EndpointsConfig": {
        "test_macvlan": {
          "IPAMConfig": {
            "IPv4Address": "192.168.1.200"
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }' \
  http://d/v5.0.0/libpod/containers/create
{"Id":"5a5203ced6672f6a9b1768fccdfc6f1ec247bc94e3d0760ae7e19814daea464c","Warnings":[]}

There is no macvlan network.



**if i use http:/libpod/containers/create, it will combine my macvlan network**

```
root@podman:/home/podman/simple-demo# curl --unix-socket /tmp/podman.sock -X POST \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "test1",
    "Image": "swr.cn-north-4.myhuaweicloud.com/ddn-k8s/gcr.io/iguazio/alpine:3.20",
    "NetworkingConfig": {
      "EndpointsConfig": {
        "test_macvlan": {
          "IPAMConfig": {
            "IPv4Address": "192.168.1.200"
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }' \
  http://libpod/containers/create
{"Id":"0db339b733bf8afa784e9ea024882dd3a2734ea03642677269168b8732640aa5","Warnings":[]}

### Describe the results you received

how can i get correct? 

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

create a correct container name with my macvlan nic

comments.body:
When used with a Unix socket, `curl` removes the hostname from the URL. For example, if you call `curl` with the URL `http://whatever/containers/create`, it makes a request to the `/containers/create` endpoint, which is mapped to the Docker-compatible API (compat API).

However, if you run this with TCP, the request returns a `404 Not Found` error because `http://localhost:8080/libpod/containers/create` doesn't exist without a version.

To fix this, we would need to add a non-versioned path to the entry point. But to call this entry point, you would need to use a URL such as `http://d/libpod/containers/create`. I am also not sure if this change would violate any compatibility requirements we need to satisfy. Furthermore, all other `libpod` endpoints are versioned. 

@Leeable I think this issue should be closed. If you have no objections, I will close it.

> When used with a Unix socket, `curl` removes the hostname from the URL. For example, if you call `curl` with the URL `http://whatever/containers/create`, it makes a request to the `/containers/create` endpoint, which is mapped to the Docker-compatible API (compat API).
> 
> However, if you run this with TCP, the request returns a `404 Not Found` error because `http://localhost:8080/libpod/containers/create` doesn't exist without a version.
> 
> To fix this, we would need to add a non-versioned path to the entry point. But to call this entry point, you would need to use a URL such as `http://d/libpod/containers/create`. I am also not sure if this change would violate any compatibility requirements we need to satisfy. Furthermore, all other `libpod` endpoints are versioned.
> 
> [@Leeable](https://github.com/Leeable) I think this issue should be closed. If you have no objections, I will close it.

> This is the [compat API](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#tag/containers-(compat)/operation/ContainerArchiveLibpod) call, which takes a container name as a query parameter.

Hi @Honny1 , so how can i get a **correct container name** with also combine my **macvlan network**?

> To fix this, we would need to add a non-versioned path to the entry point. But to call this entry point, you would need to use a URL such as http://d/libpod/containers/create. I am also not sure if this change would violate any compatibility requirements we need to satisfy. Furthermore, all other libpod endpoints are versioned.

The fact the the libpod endpoints require a version is intentional, in general it is strongly discouraged to call any endpoint with a version.

We don't really need tcp, you simply have to specify a NOP hostname like you did with `http://d/v5.0.0/libpod/containers/create`

The reason it didn't work is because you are using the payload for the docker compat endpoint against the libpod one.

for libpod you need to use the networks map to add networks to the container
